 {"id":"bd-14cg","title":"Add shared types for ToolRegistry + manifest mapping","description":"Goal:
- Define shared TypeScript types for ToolRegistry, ToolDefinition, InstallSpec, and VerificationSpec.

Considerations:
- Keep types minimal and public-safe; avoid any private infra fields.
- Prefer a single canonical type in packages/shared for reuse by gateway + web.

Definition of Done:
- Types exist in packages/shared and compile under strict TS.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:25:38.131827329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T04:26:20.585176341Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","registry","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14cg","depends_on_id":"bd-1hac","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:25:38.156665056Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14cg","depends_on_id":"bd-2k5b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:26:20.585108684Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1hac","title":"ACFS tool registry integration","description":"Background:
- Gateway setup/detection currently hardcodes tool lists and install metadata.
- ACFS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup) now owns the canonical manifest + checksums for the stack.

Goal:
- Make ACFS manifest the single source of truth for tool metadata in this public gateway repo.

Scope:
- Define mapping types, loader, registry service, and integrate into setup/detection endpoints.

Non-goals:
- Do not embed any private infrastructure or business content.
- Do not implement installers; only surface metadata + commands.

Why this matters:
- Reduces drift across repos, keeps UI and detection in sync with the actual tool stack.

Definition of Done:
- Registry service loads manifest and drives /setup readiness + tool info.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:25:36.404946939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T04:25:36.442657951Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acfs","integration","registry"]}

 {"id":"bd-1m7s","title":"Tests for NTM driver + ingest service","description":"Goal:\n- Add unit tests + mocks for NTM driver and ingest service with detailed logging assertions.\n\nScope:\n- Validate state mapping, error handling, and backoff behavior.\n- Ensure log events include correlationId, session/pane identifiers, and NTM command context.\n\nDefinition of Done:\n- Unit tests validate state mapping and error handling.\n- Logging assertions confirm structured fields are emitted.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:25:58.797304872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:08:25.643595971Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ntm","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m7s","depends_on_id":"bd-1b9b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:26:46.730349085Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7s","depends_on_id":"bd-284u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:25:58.817976113Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Document the public stack contract and integration rationale so future work is self-contained.

Definition of Done:

 {"id":"bd-3oop","title":"Integration completeness audit + gap closure","description":"Goal:
- Prove coverage by systematically auditing the integration plan against tool inventory, gateway modules, and operational concerns.

Rationale:
- Prevents silent gaps (e.g., missing tool clients, unhandled failure modes, or omitted UI flows).
- Creates a self-documenting checklist for future maintainers.

Definition of Done:
- A coverage matrix exists and every gap is converted into a bead (or explicitly marked as out-of-scope with justification).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T04:59:54.988935642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T04:59:55.140375313Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","completeness","integration"]}
